rationale: The citing paper discusses Fong, Fang, and Luo (2015) multiple times with a specific role, not just as a name in a list. It uses that study to position the gap (prior behavioral mobile-coupon evidence is limited to movie-ticket promotions and fewer features) and to motivate its broader modeling of coupon attributes in a mall setting. The citing paper also returns to Fong et al. when interpreting results, noting consistency on location-distance effects and benchmarking redemption rates. This indicates a meaningful framing and comparative function in the citing paper's contribution narrative.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the mobile coupon field-experiment stream represented by Fong et al. (2015) by extending it to a broader retail context and a richer set of coupon characteristics. It uses Fong et al. as a behavioral benchmark for location effects and redemption levels while claiming added scope and generality.
